Metadata.Tribunal_Court: High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: The Wellness Group Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: The Wellness Group Pte Ltd was established for the purposes of wholesale and/or retail of lifestyle and/or wellness related products.
TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd operated the tea division of The Wellness Group Pte Ltd.
The Wellness Group Pte Ltd, OSIM International Pte Ltd, Paris Investment Pte Ltd and TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd signed a Shareholders' Agreement dated 18 March 2011 governing TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd.
On 18 March 2011, OSIM International Pte Ltd acquired 35% of the shares in TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd, The Wellness Group Pte Ltd held 54.7%, and Paris Investment Pte Ltd held 10.3%.

Clause 5.2 of the 18 March 2011 Shareholders' Agreement provided that the board of TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd would comprise two persons appointed by Paris Investment Pte Ltd and The Wellness Group Pte Ltd, and one person appointed by OSIM International Pte Ltd for so long as OSIM International Pte Ltd's shareholding percentage was not less than 25%, with that person being Mr Ron Sim.

In December 2011, Ron Sim called for a TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd board meeting to review the suitability of Manoj Mohan Murjani continuing as chief executive officer and to consider his removal from that position, but the board meeting was not held.
On 14 August 2012, Manoj Mohan Murjani resigned as chief executive officer of TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd.
On 28 September 2012, Manoj Mohan Murjani resigned as a director of TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd and The Wellness Group Pte Ltd did not appoint anyone to replace him on the board at that time.

Subsequently, OSIM International Pte Ltd's shareholding in TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd increased to 45%, while The Wellness Group Pte Ltd's shareholding decreased to 46.3% and Paris Investment Pte Ltd's shareholding decreased to 8.7%.
On 18 October 2013, OSIM International Pte Ltd purchased all the shares in Paris Investment Pte Ltd, resulting in their combined shareholding in TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd being 53.7%.

In November 2013, TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd conducted a rights issue to raise capital and The Wellness Group Pte Ltd did not subscribe to this rights issue, while OSIM International Pte Ltd and Paris Investment Pte Ltd together subscribed for the entire rights issue, resulting in their combined shareholding being 69.9% and The Wellness Group Pte Ltd's shareholding being diluted to 30.1%.
From 2014 onwards, The Wellness Group Pte Ltd continued to hold 30.1% of the shares in TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd.

In February 2014, The Wellness Group Pte Ltd and Manoj Mohan Murjani commenced an action in Suit 187 of 2014 against OSIM International Pte Ltd, Paris Investment Pte Ltd and the directors of TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd.

On 26 October 2016, The Wellness Group Pte Ltd informed TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd that it wished to re-appoint Manoj Mohan Murjani as a director of TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd pursuant to clause 5.2 of the Shareholders' Agreement and an implied term regarding board composition.

On 1 November 2016, OSIM International Pte Ltd and Paris Investment Pte Ltd wrote to The Wellness Group Pte Ltd stating that they were unable to agree to the appointment of Manoj Mohan Murjani and indicated that they would be willing to take steps to appoint either Ms Kanchan Murjani or Mr Finian Tan, both directors of The Wellness Group Pte Ltd, if nominated.

On 3 November 2016, TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd informed The Wellness Group Pte Ltd that the board considered that appointing Manoj Mohan Murjani to the board would not be in the best interests of TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd, but stated that it was prepared to accept the appointment of either Ms Kanchan Murjani or Mr Finian Tan to the board.
After 3 November 2016, The Wellness Group Pte Ltd did not nominate either Ms Kanchan Murjani or Mr Finian Tan for appointment to the board of TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd.

In December 2016 and January 2017, The Wellness Group Pte Ltd sent several letters to TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd, OSIM International Pte Ltd and Paris Investment Pte Ltd reiterating its request to appoint Manoj Mohan Murjani as a director with immediate effect, and these requests were rejected.

On 13 February 2017, The Wellness Group Pte Ltd wrote to TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd proposing the appointment of Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee as a director of TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd and requested that he be authorised by the board to disclose information to The Wellness Group Pte Ltd in accordance with section 158 of the Companies Act and that he be covered by directors' and officers' insurance to the same extent as other directors or that such insurance be purchased.

In the 13 February 2017 letter, The Wellness Group Pte Ltd asked TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd to arrange for the appointment of Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee as a director and the requested ancillary matters to be formalised as soon as practicable.
After the 13 February 2017 letter, TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd did not send a reply to The Wellness Group Pte Ltd regarding the proposed appointment of Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee.

On 28 February 2017, The Wellness Group Pte Ltd wrote to TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd stating that the refusal to appoint Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee was a breach of the Shareholders' Agreement, that the assertion in the 23 February 2017 letter that the ancillary matters were not in the interests of TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd lacked substantiation and justification, and that there was no basis for restricting its choice of nominee director to Ms Kanchan Murjani and Mr Finian Tan.

On 23 February 2017, TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd informed The Wellness Group Pte Ltd that it would not be appointing Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee because the board was unable to accede to the two ancillary matters requested in the 13 February 2017 letter and considered those matters not to be in the interests of TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd, and reminded The Wellness Group Pte Ltd of its earlier invitations to appoint Ms Kanchan Murjani or Mr Finian Tan.

On 27 February 2017, The Wellness Group Pte Ltd filed Originating Summons No 206 of 2017 seeking reliefs relating to its entitlement to appoint a director to the board of TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd and the appointment of Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee.

On 28 February 2017, The Wellness Group Pte Ltd wrote to TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd stating that the refusal to appoint Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee was a of the Shareholders' Agreement, that the assertion in the 23 February 2017 letter that the ancillary matters were not in the interests of TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd lacked substantiation and justification, and that there was no basis for restricting its choice of nominee director to Ms Kanchan Murjani and Mr Finian Tan.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether The Wellness Group Pte Ltd could require TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd to proceed with the appointment of Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee as a director pursuant to the Shareholders' Agreement and an implied term on board composition, in circumstances where the proposal included additional requests regarding information disclosure and directors' and officers' insurance.
Party_Details.Rule: Under a shareholders' agreement that allocates board seats based on shareholding percentages, a shareholder with a specified minimum shareholding may have a contractual right to nominate a person for appointment as director, while the company and other shareholders consider the nomination and any accompanying terms in light of the agreement and the company's interests.
Party_Details.Application: The Wellness Group Pte Ltd held 30.1% of the shares in TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd and, together with the terms of the 18 March 2011 Shareholders' Agreement and the implied term on board composition identified in earlier proceedings, treated this shareholding as supporting a right to nominate one director. After a period without exercising this right following the resignation of Manoj Mohan Murjani, The Wellness Group Pte Ltd first sought to have him re-appointed and, when that was not accepted, proposed Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee as a director. The proposal for Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee included requests that he be authorised to disclose company information to The Wellness Group Pte Ltd under section 158 of the Companies Act and that he be covered by directors' and officers' insurance on the same basis as other directors or that such insurance be purchased. TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd and the majority shareholders did not accept the nomination on those terms and invited The Wellness Group Pte Ltd instead to nominate either Ms Kanchan Murjani or Mr Finian Tan, which The Wellness Group Pte Ltd did not do. The Wellness Group Pte Ltd then commenced Originating Summons No 206 of 2017 seeking declarations and orders to give effect to the appointment of Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log:
The judge described the application as being based on the 18 March 2011 Shareholders' Agreement and an implied term identified in earlier litigation that the majority shareholder or shareholders would be entitled to appoint two directors to the board of TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd and the minority shareholder or shareholders holding at least 25% of the shares would be entitled to appoint one director. The judge noted that The Wellness Group Pte Ltd had ceased to be the majority shareholder and that OSIM International Pte Ltd together with Paris Investment Pte Ltd formed the majority shareholders, while The Wellness Group Pte Ltd, as minority shareholder, held 30.1% of the shares and was therefore entitled under the implied term to appoint one director. The judge recorded that the application before the court sought, among other things, a declaration of this entitlement and an order that Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee be appointed as a director, together with ancillary orders to give effect to the appointment.

In analysing whether the defendants had breached the implied term, the judge stated that it was common ground that The Wellness Group Pte Ltd had a contractual right to appoint a director pursuant to the implied term and that this right was, in effect, a right to nominate a person to be appointed as a director. The judge recorded that The Wellness Group Pte Ltd conceded that it was not entitled to impose conditions in respect of Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee's appointment, but argued that the ancillary matters in the 13 February 2017 letter were merely requests and that the point was moot because those matters were not part of the prayers in the present proceedings. The Wellness Group Pte Ltd contended that, since the unacceptability of the ancillary matters was the only reason given for not appointing Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee, the defendants had no other basis to object once those matters were no longer pursued.

The judge rejected this characterisation and concluded that The Wellness Group Pte Ltd had changed its position with respect to the ancillary matters. The judge held that the ancillary matters were clearly intended and conveyed as conditions attached to the appointment, relying on the wording of paragraph 7 of the 13 February 2017 letter, which asked that the appointment and the ancillary matters be formalised together, and paragraph 3 of the 17 February 2017 letter, which requested steps to formalise the appointment including the ancillary matters. The judge also relied on the 23 February 2017 letter from TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd, which stated that the board would not be appointing Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee because the ancillary matters were not in the interests of the company, and observed that The Wellness Group Pte Ltd did not clarify at that time that the ancillary matters were merely requests or that the appointment could proceed without them. The judge further noted that there was nothing in the affidavit filed on 27 February 2017 on behalf of The Wellness Group Pte Ltd explaining that the ancillary matters were only requests, and that even in its 28 February 2017 reply, The Wellness Group Pte Ltd continued to challenge the assertion that the ancillary matters were not in the company's interests without indicating that it was no longer insisting on them. The judge stated that the first clarification that The Wellness Group Pte Ltd was not insisting on the ancillary matters appeared only in its written submissions exchanged on 6 July 2017, two working days before the hearing, and commented that this seemed calculated to gain a tactical advantage.

The defendants submitted that they had considered the appointment of Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee on the basis that the ancillary matters were conditions and that the TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd board refused the appointment because those conditions were unacceptable. They argued that they did not have to and did not consider whether he should be appointed on the basis that the ancillary matters could be disregarded, and that they should be given an opportunity to reconsider the appointment afresh without those conditions. The judge accepted this submission and held that the defendants were entitled to reconsider the appointment of Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee on the basis that the ancillary matters were not conditions attached to his appointment. On that basis, the judge dismissed The Wellness Group Pte Ltd's application and ordered it to pay fixed costs of $5,000 (including disbursements) to TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd and $5,000 (including disbursements) to OSIM International Pte Ltd and Paris Investment Pte Ltd.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd operates Wellness' tea division.
In early 2011, the then-director and chief executive officer of TWG Tea, Mr Manoj Mohan Murjani, started negotiations with Mr Ron Sim Chye Hock regarding an investment by OSIM into TWG Tea.
In early 2011, the then-director and chief executive officer of TWG Tea, Mr Manoj Mohan Murjani, started negotiations with Mr Ron Sim Chye Hock regarding an investment by OSIM into TWG Tea.
On 18 March 2011, Wellness, OSIM, Paris and TWG Tea signed the SHA, which provided for the composition of TWG Tea's board of directors.
On 18 March 2011, OSIM acquired 35% of the shares in TWG Tea, while Wellness and Paris held 54.7% and 10.3% respectively.

In December 2011, Ron Sim called for a TWG Tea board meeting to review the suitability of Manoj continuing as CEO and if thought appropriate to remove him as CEO; for various reasons, the board meeting was not held.
On 14 August 2012, Manoj Mohan Murjani resigned as chief executive officer of TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd.
On 28 September 2012, Manoj Mohan Murjani resigned as a director of TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd and no replacement director from The Wellness Group Pte Ltd was appointed at that time.
Subsequently, OSIM's shareholding in TWG Tea increased to 45% while Wellness' and Paris' shareholdings decreased to 46.3% and 8.7% respectively.
On 18 October 2013, OSIM purchased all the shares in Paris. Their combined shareholding in TWG Tea was 53.7%.

In November 2013, TWG Tea had a rights issue to raise capital. Wellness did not subscribe to this rights issue. OSIM and Paris together subscribed for the entire rights issue. Consequently, OSIM and Paris, combined, held 69.9% of the shares in TWG Tea while Wellness' shareholding was diluted to 30.1%.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd was required under the Shareholders' Agreement and the implied term on board composition to proceed with the appointment of Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee as a director based on The Wellness Group Pte Ltd's nomination, notwithstanding the inclusion of additional requests concerning information disclosure and directors' and officers' insurance.
Party_Details.Rule: Where a shareholders' agreement grants a shareholder a contractual right to nominate a director, the company and its board consider the nomination in light of the agreement, any implied terms on board composition, and the company's interests, and may assess any additional conditions or requests attached to the nomination when deciding whether to proceed with the appointment.
Party_Details.Application: TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd was the 18 March 2011 Shareholders' Agreement, which, together with an implied term identified in earlier litigation, allocated board seats between majority and minority shareholders based on shareholding percentages. After The Wellness Group Pte Ltd's shareholding was diluted to 30.1%, it treated itself as entitled to nominate one director and, following the resignation of Manoj Mohan Murjani, sought first to have him re-appointed and later to have Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee appointed. The nomination of Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee was accompanied by requests that he be authorised to disclose company information to The Wellness Group Pte Ltd under section 158 of the Companies Act and that he be covered by directors' and officers' insurance on the same basis as other directors or that such insurance be purchased. TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd did not respond immediately to the initial proposal and, after follow-up letters, stated on 23 February 2017 that it would not appoint Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee because the board was unable to accede to the requested ancillary matters and considered them not to be in the company's interests, while reiterating its willingness to accept either Ms Kanchan Murjani or Mr Finian Tan as a director. The Wellness Group Pte Ltd did not nominate those individuals and instead commenced Originating Summons No 206 of 2017 seeking declarations and orders to compel the appointment of Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log:
The judge accepted that TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd was operating under a shareholders' agreement and an implied term that allocated board seats between majority and minority shareholders, and that The Wellness Group Pte Ltd, as minority shareholder with 30.1% of the shares, had a right to nominate one director. The judge emphasised that this right was a right to nominate rather than an automatic right to have a particular individual appointed. In assessing the events surrounding the nomination of Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee, the judge concluded that the ancillary matters in the 13 February 2017 letter were presented as conditions attached to the appointment and that TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd's board had considered the nomination on that basis.

The judge found that TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd's 23 February 2017 letter clearly stated that the board would not appoint Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee because the ancillary matters were not in the company's interests, and that The Wellness Group Pte Ltd did not, at that time or in its supporting affidavit, indicate that it was willing to proceed with the appointment without those matters. The judge noted that The Wellness Group Pte Ltd only clarified in its written submissions shortly before the hearing that it was not insisting on the ancillary matters. The defendants, including TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd, argued that they had not considered the nomination on the basis that the ancillary matters could be disregarded and that they should be given an opportunity to reconsider the appointment afresh without those conditions. The judge agreed with this position and held that the defendants were entitled to reconsider the appointment of Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee on the new basis advanced by The Wellness Group Pte Ltd. On that footing, the judge dismissed the application and ordered The Wellness Group Pte Ltd to pay fixed costs to TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd and to OSIM International Pte Ltd and Paris Investment Pte Ltd.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: OSIM International Pte Ltd
Counsel: Davinder Singh s/o Amar Singh, SC

Facts.Text: OSIM International Pte Ltd was a public company listed on the Singapore Stock Exchange, and Ron Sim Chye Hock was its CEO, a director and Chairman.
In early 2011, Manoj Mohan Murjani started negotiations with Ron Sim regarding an investment by OSIM into TWG Tea.
On 18 March 2011, OSIM acquired 35% of the shares in TWG Tea.
On 18 March 2011, OSIM, Wellness, Paris and TWG Tea signed the SHA.
In December 2011, Ron Sim called for a TWG Tea board meeting to review the suitability of Manoj continuing as CEO and if thought appropriate to remove him as CEO.
Subsequently, OSIM’s shareholding in TWG Tea increased to 45% while Wellness’ and Paris’ shareholdings decreased to 46.3% and 8.7% respectively.
On 18 October 2013, OSIM purchased all the shares in Paris. Their combined shareholding in TWG Tea was 53.7%.

In November 2013, TWG Tea had a rights issue to raise capital. Wellness did not subscribe to this rights issue. OSIM and Paris together subscribed for the entire rights issue. Consequently, OSIM and Paris, combined, held 69.9% of the shares in TWG Tea while Wellness’ shareholding was diluted to 30.1%.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether OSIM International Pte Ltd, as a majority shareholder of TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd under the Shareholders' Agreement and implied term on board composition, was obliged to support the appointment of Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee as a director based on The Wellness Group Pte Ltd's nomination and accompanying requests.
Party_Details.Rule: Under a shareholders' agreement that allocates board representation between majority and minority shareholders, majority shareholders participate in decisions on director appointments in accordance with the agreement and any implied terms, and may evaluate a minority shareholder's nominee and any attached conditions in light of the company's interests and the contractual framework.
Party_Details.Application: OSIM International Pte Ltd acquired a significant shareholding in TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd in March 2011 and, after subsequent transactions including the purchase of all shares in Paris Investment Pte Ltd and participation in a rights issue, held a majority interest in TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd together with Paris Investment Pte Ltd. The 18 March 2011 Shareholders' Agreement and an implied term identified in earlier litigation allocated two board seats to the majority shareholder or shareholders and one board seat to the minority shareholder or shareholders holding at least 25% of the shares. When The Wellness Group Pte Ltd, as minority shareholder with 30.1% of the shares, sought to re-appoint Manoj Mohan Murjani as a director, OSIM International Pte Ltd and Paris Investment Pte Ltd stated on 1 November 2016 that they could not agree to his appointment and indicated their willingness to support the appointment of either Ms Kanchan Murjani or Mr Finian Tan. Later, when The Wellness Group Pte Ltd proposed Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee as a director and requested authorisation for him to disclose company information and to be covered by directors' and officers' insurance, OSIM International Pte Ltd, as part of the majority shareholder group, did not agree to proceed with the appointment on those terms and maintained its position that it would support alternative nominees from The Wellness Group Pte Ltd.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log:
The judge treated OSIM International Pte Ltd, together with Paris Investment Pte Ltd, as forming the majority shareholders of TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd after the changes in shareholding and applied the implied term from earlier litigation that the majority shareholder or shareholders would be entitled to appoint two directors and the minority shareholder or shareholders holding at least 25% of the shares would be entitled to appoint one director. The judge accepted that The Wellness Group Pte Ltd, as minority shareholder with 30.1% of the shares, had a right to nominate one director but emphasised that this was a right to nominate rather than an automatic right to have a particular individual appointed.

In considering the conduct of OSIM International Pte Ltd and Paris Investment Pte Ltd, the judge noted that they had indicated their willingness to support the appointment of either Ms Kanchan Murjani or Mr Finian Tan as a director nominated by The Wellness Group Pte Ltd, but did not agree to the appointment of Manoj Mohan Murjani. When The Wellness Group Pte Ltd later proposed Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee with ancillary requests regarding information disclosure and directors' and officers' insurance, the judge accepted the defendants' position that they had considered the nomination on the basis that these ancillary matters were conditions attached to the appointment and that the TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd board refused the appointment because those conditions were unacceptable. The judge concluded that the defendants, including OSIM International Pte Ltd, were entitled to reconsider the nomination of Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee afresh once The Wellness Group Pte Ltd indicated that it was no longer insisting on the ancillary matters, and on that basis dismissed the application and ordered The Wellness Group Pte Ltd to pay costs.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Paris Investment Pte Ltd
Counsel: Davinder Singh s/o Amar Singh, SC

Facts.Text: On 18 March 2011, OSIM International Pte Ltd acquired 35% of the shares in TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd. The other two shareholders, The Wellness Group Pte Ltd and Paris Investment Pte Ltd, held 54.7% and 10.3% respectively.

On 18 March 2011, Paris Investment Pte Ltd signed a Shareholders' Agreement with The Wellness Group Pte Ltd, OSIM International Pte Ltd and TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd governing the affairs and board composition of TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd.

Subsequently, OSIM International Pte Ltd's shareholding in TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd increased to 45%, while The Wellness Group Pte Ltd's and Paris Investment Pte Ltd's shareholdings decreased to 46.3% and 8.7% respectively.

On 18 October 2013, OSIM International Pte Ltd purchased all the shares in Paris Investment Pte Ltd, resulting in OSIM International Pte Ltd and Paris Investment Pte Ltd together holding 53.7% of the shares in TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd.

In November 2013, Paris Investment Pte Ltd and OSIM International Pte Ltd together subscribed for the entire rights issue conducted by TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd, resulting in their combined shareholding being 69.9% and The Wellness Group Pte Ltd's shareholding being diluted to 30.1%.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Paris Investment Pte Ltd, as part of the majority shareholder group of TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd under the Shareholders' Agreement and implied term on board composition, was support the appointment of Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee as a director based on The Wellness Group Pte Ltd's nomination and accompanying requests.
Party_Details.Rule: In a company governed by a shareholders' agreement that allocates board seats between majority and minority shareholders, a shareholder forming part of the majority group participates in decisions on director appointments in accordance with the agreement and any implied terms, and may consider the suitability of a minority shareholder's nominee and any attached conditions in light of the company's interests.
Party_Details.Application: Paris Investment Pte Ltd initially held 10.3% of the shares in TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd and later, after changes in shareholdings and its acquisition by OSIM International Pte Ltd, formed part of the majority shareholder group that held 69.9% of the shares following the November 2013 rights issue. Under the 18 March 2011 Shareholders' Agreement and an implied term identified in earlier litigation, the majority shareholder or shareholders were entitled to appoint two directors and the minority shareholder or shareholders holding at least 25% of the shares were entitled to appoint one director. When The Wellness Group Pte Ltd, as minority shareholder with 30.1% of the shares, sought to re-appoint Manoj Mohan Murjani as a director, Paris Investment Pte Ltd and OSIM International Pte Ltd stated on 1 November 2016 that they could not agree to his appointment and indicated their willingness to support the appointment of either Ms Kanchan Murjani or Mr Finian Tan. Later, when The Wellness Group Pte Ltd proposed Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee as a director with additional requests regarding information disclosure and directors' and officers' insurance, Paris Investment Pte Ltd, as part of the majority shareholder group, did not support proceeding with the appointment on those terms and maintained its position that it would support alternative nominees from The Wellness Group Pte Ltd.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log:
The judge regarded Paris Investment Pte Ltd, together with OSIM International Pte Ltd, as forming the majority shareholders of TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd after the changes in shareholding and applied the implied term from earlier litigation that the majority shareholder or shareholders would be entitled to appoint two directors and the minority shareholder or shareholders holding at least 25% of the shares would be entitled to appoint one director. The judge accepted that The Wellness Group Pte Ltd, as minority shareholder with 30.1% of the shares, had a right to nominate one director but emphasised that this was a right to nominate rather than an automatic right to have a particular individual appointed.

The judge noted that Paris Investment Pte Ltd and OSIM International Pte Ltd had indicated their willingness to support the appointment of either Ms Kanchan Murjani or Mr Finian Tan as a director nominated by The Wellness Group Pte Ltd, but did not agree to the appointment of Manoj Mohan Murjani. When The Wellness Group Pte Ltd later proposed Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee with ancillary requests regarding information disclosure and directors' and officers' insurance, the judge accepted the defendants' submission that they had considered the nomination on the basis that these ancillary matters were conditions attached to the appointment and that the TWG Tea Company Pte Ltd board refused the appointment because those conditions were unacceptable. The judge concluded that the defendants, including Paris Investment Pte Ltd, were entitled to reconsider the nomination of Associate Professor Mak Yuen Tee afresh once The Wellness Group Pte Ltd indicated that it was no longer insisting on the ancillary matters, and on that basis dismissed the application and ordered The Wellness Group Pte Ltd to pay costs.
